title: The Passport (Amendment) Rules, 1998 (05.10.1998)
text: REGD. NO. D.L.-33004/98 The Gazette of India EXTRAORDINARY PART II—Section 3— Sub-section (i) PUBLISHED BY AUTHORITY No. 385 1 NEW DELHI, THURSDAY, OCTOBER 8,1998/ASVINA16,1920 2659 GI/98 (1) -- 1 of 4 -- 2 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA; EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(i)] MINISTRY OF EXTERNAL AFFAIRS NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 5th October, 1998 G.S.R 605 (K).—In exercise of the powers conferred by section 24, read with section 5 of the Passports Act, 1967 (15 of 1967), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules further to amend the passport Rules, 1980, namely 1. (1) These Rules may be called the passports (Amendment) Rulcs, 1998, (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2 In the Passsport Rules, 1980 (hereinafter referred to as the principal rules), in rule 12 for sub-ruled), the following sub rules shall be substituted namely :— "(1) An ordinary passport for persons other than children below the age of 15 years, containing thirty-six pages or sixty pages shall be in force for a period of 10 years or 20 years as the case may be, from the date of its issue. (I A) An ordinary passport for a child below the age of 15 years, containing thirty-six pages shall be in force for a period of 5 years from the date of its issue or until the child attains the age of 15 years, whichever is earlier." -- 2 of 4 -- 3 3. In Schedule IV to the principal rules:- (a) for serial No. 1 and the entries relating thereto, the following shall be substituted namely :- 1 "1. 1a lb. 1c. 1d. 2 Ordinary passport containing thirty six pages with a maximum validity often years (no extra fee will be charged for renewals upto ten years if initial validity is less). Ordinary passport containing thirty six pages with a maximum validity of twenty years (no extra fees will be charged for renewals upto twenty years if initial validity is less). Ordinary passport containing sixty pages valid for ten years (no extra fees will be charged for renewals upto ten years if initial validity is less). Ordinary passport containing sixty pages valid for twenty years (no extra fees will be charged for renewals upto twenty years if initial validity is less) Ordinary passport containing thirty six pages with a maximum validity of five years for minor children below the age of 15 years. 3 Rs. 300/- Rs.600/- Rs. 500/- Rs. 800/- Rs. 200/- 4 Rs. 750/- Rs. 1500/- Rs. 1000/- Rs. 1750/- Rs. 500/- (b) for serial No. 14 and the entries relating thereto, the following shall be substituted namely:- 1 14. 14a. 2 Miscellaneous services requiring issue of fresh passport booklet containing thirty six pages. Miscellaneous services requiring issue of fresh passport booklet containing sixty pages. 3 Rs. 300/- Rs. 500/- 4 Rs. 750/- Rs. 1000/-" (c) for serial No. 21 and the entries relating thereto, the following shall be substituted namely:- 1 "21 2 Issue of duplicate passport in liue of passport which has been lost, stolen, damaged, destroyed or for any other reason. 3 Rs. 2500/- 4 Rs. 6500/-" [File No. VI/401/39/93] FRANCIS VA2, Jt. Secy. Note :—Principal rules published vide Notification No. GSR 697 (E) dated December, 11,1980 (1980 external) Part II, Section 3, sub-section (i), subsequently amended by:— 1. Notification No. GSR 50 (E) dated February 7,1981. 2. Notification No. GSR 469 (E) dated June 24, 1982. 3. Notification No. GSR 325(E) dated April 12, 1983. 4. Notification No. GSR 494(E) dated June 16, 1983. 5. Notification No. GSR 345 (E) dated March 29, 1985. 6. Notification No. GSR 860 (E) dated November 1, 1985. 7. Notification No. GSR 1155 (E) dated November 21,1988. 8. Notification No. GSR 100 (E) dated March 4, 1991. 9. Notification No. GSR 503 (E) dated July 10, 1993. 10. Notification No. GSR 622 (E) dated June 27, 1990.
